economic_notes: Malahat Nation has extensive business enterprises including aquaculture, construction, forestry, land development, fuels, and a business park. Related party transactions include accounting services, construction projects, and cardlock construction/management services.
notes: Audit is qualified because financial information for related government business enterprises and partnerships (22 entities) is not available and has not been fully accounted for. Long-term debt includes both All Nations Trust Company loans ($3,672,476) and FNFA promissory notes ($38,252,830) and capital lease obligations ($28,138). Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments on long-term debt ($148,718) plus promissory notes ($1,207,341) plus capital lease ($10,208) = $1,366,267. Total government transfers include ISC, CMHC, DFO, Transport Canada, Government of Canada Other, Parks Canada, FNHA, Province of BC, Natural Resources Canada, ESDC ($9,369,224). Prior year comparatives were restated to reflect recognition of net income from GBEs as Salish Strait Seafoods financial information became available.
